behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often come from actual STT provider behavior/model settings over real API calls (e.g., diarization off, label drift), which mocks cannot faithfully expose.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against a real recorded meeting audio using the real STT API over HTTP; assert returned transcript contains stable speaker labels and that those labels are present in the summarizer input mapping.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Assignment extraction can be validated as deterministic parsing/transform logic from transcript text to structured fields without I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures containing explicit assignments (e.g., 'Alex, please send the draft by Friday') and assert structured output includes correct assignee and normalized task text.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Coverage of assigned attendees in generated action items depends on real LLM generation quality and non-deterministic omissions.
behaviors.test_description: Send a transcript with known per-attendee assignments to the production summarization model and verify output action_items include at least one item per assigned attendee across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: If routing is implemented as deterministic schema-mapping logic, failures are observable at function/module boundary without external systems.
behaviors.test_description: Provide structured intermediate data containing detected decisions and assert mapper places them in `decisions[]` and not in narrative/notes fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Whether decisions are surfaced in the correct section is strongly dependent on live LLM adherence to prompt/schema and can fail despite correct deterministic code.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts containing explicit decision statements and assert those decisions appear in `decisions` (not only narrative text), evaluating multiple runs for consistency.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans chunking orchestration plus multiple sequential STT/LLM passes and merge quality across segments; real failures emerge from cross-step interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Process a real >60-minute meeting recording through end-to-end segmented mode; verify segment boundaries were processed in order and final merged summary preserves cross-segment continuity (actions/decisions not duplicated or dropped).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: By definition this is an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with external STT and LLM steps; only full workflow execution can expose orchestration and handoff failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run from audio upload through delivery of final structured JSON for a real meeting file, asserting successful completion, schema validity, and no manual retries/interventions.
